questions.q: Monomers are the building blocks of what?
questions.explanation: Monomers are small molecules that combine to form large molecules called polymers.

questions.q: Which type of polymer is used in making plastic bags?
questions.explanation: Thermoplastic polymers are used in making plastic bags.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a thermosetting polymer?
questions.explanation: Bakelite is an example of a thermosetting polymer.

questions.q: What is the process of breaking down large polymer molecules into smaller ones called?
questions.explanation: Depolymerization is the process of breaking down large polymer molecules into smaller ones.

questions.q: Which of the following is a natural polymer?
What is the difference between a homopolymer and a copolymer?
opts: (A) Homopolymer is made from two different monomers and copolymer is made from one monomer.
(B) Homopolymer is made from one type of monomer and copolymer is made from two or more different monomers.
(C) Homopolymer is biodegradable and copolymer is not.
(D) Homopolymer is non-biodegradable and copolymer is biodegradable.
questions.explanation: A homopolymer is made from one type of monomer, whereas a copolymer is made from two or more different monomers.

questions.q: What is the term for the arrangement of polymer chains in a regular and repeating pattern?
questions.explanation: Crystallization is the term for the arrangement of polymer chains in a regular and repeating pattern.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an elastomer?
questions.explanation: Rubber is an example of an elastomer.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of adding a small molecule to the end of a polymer chain?
questions.explanation: Termination is the term for the process of adding a small molecule to the end of a polymer chain.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a thermoplastic polymer?
opts: (A) It can be melted and reformed multiple times.
questions.explanation: A thermoplastic polymer can be melted and reformed multiple times.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of breaking a polymer chain into smaller fragments?
questions.explanation: Depolymerization is the term for the process of breaking a polymer chain into smaller fragments.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of polymerization reaction?
questions.explanation: Both addition and condensation polymerization are types of polymerization reactions.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of adding a small molecule to the end of a polymer chain in a condensation polymerization reaction?
questions.explanation: Termination is the term for the process of adding a small molecule to the end of a polymer chain in a condensation polymerization reaction.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a polymer?
opts: (A) It has a high melting point and is brittle.
(B) It has a low melting point and is flexible.
questions.explanation: A polymer typically has a low melting point and is flexible.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of creating a new polymer by combining two or more monomers?
questions.explanation: Copolymerization is the term for the process of creating a new polymer by combining two or more monomers.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a biodegradable polymer?
questions.explanation: Starch-based polymers are examples of biodegradable polymers.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of creating a new polymer by combining two or more monomers with different functional groups?
questions.explanation: Copolymerization is the term for the process of creating a new polymer by combining two or more monomers with different functional groups.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a thermosetting polymer?
opts: (A) It can be melted and reformed multiple times.
(D) It is rigid and cannot be melted after hardening.
questions.explanation: A thermosetting polymer is rigid and cannot be melted after hardening.

questions.q: What is the term for the arrangement of polymer chains in a random and irregular pattern?
questions.explanation: An amorphous solid is the term for the arrangement of polymer chains in a random and irregular pattern.
